headline: Mandatory Content Filtering on School Networks Blocks 40% of Education
context: Ministry-mandated content filtering on school networks inadvertently blocked 40% of legitimate educational websites in 2025.

cards.big: School content filters blocked 40% of legitimate educational websites
cards.sub: Overly broad filtering prevented access to science history and health content

cards.big: 12,000 teachers reported inability to access lesson plan resources
cards.sub: Wikipedia blocked intermittently due to content flagging algorithms

cards.big: Students could not access Khan Academy and 28 other educational platforms
cards.sub: MCMC filtering categories applied to schools designed for general public use

cards.big: Teachers must submit individual unblocking requests taking 2-4 weeks each
cards.sub: Only 180 of 4,500 unblocking requests processed by end of 2025

cards.big: Education filtering tests whether child protection tools account for learning access needs
40% of educational content blocked by mandatory school filters. Over-blocking defeats the purpose of digital learning.
